perturbation.original_text: It is understood and agreed that by reason of this "Agreement" the "Parties" that are involved during the course of business transactions may learn from one another, or from the <PAGE> 2 principals the names, addresses, telephone numbers of lenders, agents, brokers, clients or others hereafter referred to as "Contracts" and or "Associates".
perturbation.changed_text: It is understood and agreed that by reason of this Agreement, the parties may learn information from one another.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific types of contacts ('lenders, agents, brokers, clients, etc.') whose information is protected as 'Contracts' or 'Associates,' the scope of protection becomes unclear. This creates an in-text contradiction because it undermines the non-circumvention clause, since it is no longer clear who is considered a protected contact. This omission significantly impacts sections 1f where contracts are and shall be recognized as exclusive and valuable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood and agreed that the "Contracts" of each party hereto are and shall be recognized as exclusive and valuable "Contracts" and that the parties will not directly or indirectly negotiate or participate in any transaction circumventing the party who first provided the "Contract".
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific types of contacts ('lenders, agents, brokers, clients, etc.') whose information is protected, which creates an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which explicitly states that 'Contracts' of each party are recognized as exclusive and valuable. By removing the specific definition of 'Contracts', the changed text makes the non-circumvention clause in the contradicted text unclear and unenforceable, as it's no longer clear who is considered a protected contact. This follows the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute arising out of this Agreement shall be adjudicated in arbitration under the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The prevailing party in any dispute shall be reimbursed reasonable attorneys fees.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute arising out of this Agreement shall be resolved through amicable discussion.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the arbitration clause and the provision for reimbursing attorney's fees significantly weakens the enforceability of the agreement. Changing to 'amicable discussion' leaves the dispute resolution process vague, which creates contradiction with the fact that this is a legal agreement and makes litigation not straight forward. It contradicts section 12: 'This Agreement is a full recourse agreement concluded under the laws of Pennsylvania and said forum shall be applicable law covering the construction, interpretation, execution, validity, enforceability, performance, and any other such matters in respect to this Agreement, including any breach or claim of breach hereof'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is a full recourse agreement concluded under the laws of Pennsylvania and said forum shall be applicable law covering the construction, interpretation, execution, validity, enforceability, performance, and any other such matters in respect to this Agreement, including any breach or claim of breach hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified arbitration for dispute resolution, ensuring enforceability. The changed text replaces this with 'amicable discussion,' a vague process that contradicts the 'full recourse agreement' clause in the contradicted text. This contradiction arises because 'amicable discussion' lacks the legal enforceability implied by 'full recourse agreement' and a specific forum (Pennsylvania law), making the agreement's enforceability unclear and inconsistent, falling under the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction.'
